title: Xtra Silky Peruvian Grape Leaves Dumplings
description: A winey, silky, and savory Peruvian dish featuring grape leaves, perfect for midnight cravings, made diabetic-friendly with careful ingredient selection. This recipe is a unique twist on traditional Peruvian cuisine.
instructions: Rinse the grape leaves and remove the stems, then set them aside.
In a large mortar, pound the chopped onion and minced garlic until well combined.
Add the ground beef to the mortar and pound until just combined, being careful not to overwork the meat.
Stir in the white wine, olive oil, salt, black pepper, and tomato paste.
Lay a grape leaf flat on a work surface, with the stem end facing you, and place about 1 tablespoon of the meat mixture in the center of the leaf.
Fold the stem end over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the leaf into a neat package, repeating with the remaining leaves and filling.
In a large pot, combine the chicken broth and the rolled grape leaves, then bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the grape leaves are tender and the filling is cooked through.
Serve the dumplings hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F.
